name: Franchise Renewal Agreement
summary: Drafts a comprehensive Franchise Renewal Agreement to extend an existing franchise relationship between franchisor and franchisee. Protects franchisor's intellectual property and brand standards while setting clear renewal conditions, performance requirements, and compliance terms to minimize disputes. Use when preparing renewal documents based on original agreement review and franchisee performance history.
content:
# FRANCHISE RENEWAL AGREEMENT DRAFTING PROMPT

## Your Role and Objective

You are an experienced franchise attorney tasked with drafting a comprehensive Franchise Renewal Agreement that extends an existing franchise relationship between a franchisor and franchisee. This transactional document must achieve a delicate balance: protecting the franchisor's intellectual property, brand standards, and system uniformity while providing the franchisee with clear expectations and a viable path to continue their established business operations. Your draft must be precise, enforceable, and structured to minimize future disputes by eliminating ambiguity in renewal conditions, performance standards, and compliance requirements.

## Initial Information Gathering and Document Review

Before beginning your draft, conduct a thorough review of all available materials related to this franchise relationship. Search through any uploaded documents to identify the original franchise agreement, including its execution date, current term expiration date, and all material terms governing the existing relationship. Extract specific details such as the franchisee's legal name, entity type, franchise identification number, franchised location address, current royalty rates, territorial rights, and any special provisions or amendments that have modified the original agreement. Identify the franchisor's current standard form franchise agreement to understand what terms will govern the renewal period, paying particular attention to any material changes in royalty structures, advertising contributions, operational requirements, or territorial restrictions.

Review the franchisee's performance history under the original agreement by examining any compliance reports, default notices, cure correspondence, or performance evaluations that may exist in the available documents. Determine whether all financial obligations are current, whether the franchisee has maintained required insurance coverage, and whether any material defaults have occurred during the original term. This historical context will inform the conditions precedent section and help you assess whether additional protective provisions are warranted based on the franchisee's track record.

## Document Structure and Opening Provisions

Begin your draft with a centered, capitalized title "FRANCHISE RENEWAL AGREEMENT" followed by a comprehensive opening paragraph that establishes the parties' complete legal identities. Identify the franchisor with its full legal name, entity type (corporation, limited liability company, partnership), state of organization, and principal place of business. Similarly identify the franchisee with complete legal information, including the franchise identification number and the specific street address of the franchised location being renewed. Specify the effective date of this renewal agreement, which should be carefully coordinated with the expiration date of the original agreement to ensure seamless transition without gaps in coverage.

Draft detailed recitals that establish both the factual foundation and legal context for this renewal. Your first recital should reference the original franchise agreement with its complete execution date and briefly describe the franchise rights initially granted, including the franchised location and any territorial protections. The second recital must specify the exact expiration date of the current term and confirm that the parties are executing this renewal agreement in advance of that expiration to ensure business continuity. Your third recital should affirmatively state that the franchisee has expressed its desire to renew the franchise relationship and that the franchisor has agreed to such renewal subject to the terms and conditions contained herein. Consider adding a fourth recital that acknowledges the franchisee's performance history and the mutual benefits of continuing the franchise relationship, which can provide helpful context if disputes later arise regarding the parties' intentions and expectations.

## Conditions Precedent to Renewal Effectiveness

Draft a comprehensive section establishing all conditions that the franchisee must satisfy before the renewal becomes legally effective. Structure this as clear, flowing prose that guides the reader through each requirement logically. The franchisee must demonstrate full compliance with all material terms of the original agreement throughout its entire term, with no uncured defaults existing as of the renewal effective date. Specify that the franchisee must have provided timely written notice of intent to renew within the timeframe specified in the original agreement, typically between six and twelve months prior to expiration, and include the actual date by which such notice was required and confirm it was received.

Address the franchisee's financial standing by requiring that all amounts owed to the franchisor must be current with no outstanding arrearages, including royalties, advertising fund contributions, technology fees, audit assessments, and any other financial obligations. Require that the franchisee has continuously maintained all required insurance coverage with the franchisor named as an additional insured, all necessary business licenses and permits, and all health and safety certifications required to operate the franchised business. Include a condition requiring the franchisee to execute a general release of claims against the franchisor, its affiliates, officers, directors, employees, and agents, releasing all claims arising from or related to the franchise relationship from its inception through the date of renewal, with appropriate carve-outs for claims that cannot legally be released under applicable state franchise relationship laws.

Require that the franchisee and all of its principal owners execute the franchisor's then-current standard form of franchise agreement, personal guarantees if required, and any ancillary agreements such as lease assignments, non-disclosure agreements, or technology licensing agreements. Specify that the franchisee must complete all required premises upgrades, remodeling, and modernization work to bring the franchised location into compliance with current brand standards, subject to the detailed requirements set forth in a subsequent section. Include a condition that the franchisee must successfully complete any required retraining programs for the franchisee and its management personnel on current operational procedures, new menu items or service offerings, and updated technology systems that have been implemented since the original agreement was executed.

## Financial Consideration and Renewal Fee Structure

Establish the financial consideration for the renewal in clear, unambiguous terms that leave no room for interpretation or dispute. Specify the exact renewal fee amount as a fixed dollar figure rather than a formula or percentage, and indicate whether this fee is in addition to or inclusive of any initial franchise fee that may be required under the new franchise agreement. Detail the payment terms with precision, including the exact due date for the renewal fee (typically upon execution of this renewal agreement or within a specified number of days thereafter), acceptable payment methods (wire transfer, certified check, or other specified forms), and the consequences for late payment or non-payment, which should include the franchisor's right to terminate this renewal agreement and refuse to extend the franchise relationship.

Address whether the renewal fee is refundable under any circumstances, and if refundability is contemplated, specify those circumstances with absolute precision, including the timeframe within which refund requests must be made and any conditions that would forfeit refund rights. Include language that characterizes the renewal fee as separate and independent consideration for the franchisor's agreement to extend the franchise relationship and grant the franchisee the continued right to use the franchisor's proprietary marks, system, and confidential information for an additional term. Consider whether the renewal fee should be credited against any initial franchise fee required under the new franchise agreement, or whether these represent distinct forms of consideration for different aspects of the renewed relationship.

## Terms Governing the Renewal Period

Draft comprehensive provisions addressing the franchise agreement that will govern the renewal term and the relationship between this renewal agreement and the new franchise agreement. Clearly state that upon satisfaction of all renewal conditions and payment of the renewal fee, the parties shall execute the franchisor's then-current standard form of franchise agreement, which may contain terms, conditions, royalty rates, advertising contributions, technology fees, and operational obligations that differ materially from those in the original agreement. Specify the length of the renewal term, whether it matches the duration of the original term or differs, and address whether any additional renewal rights will be granted beyond this renewal term or whether this represents the final extension of the franchise relationship.

Include language acknowledging that the franchisee has been provided with a copy of or has had access to review the current form of franchise agreement that will govern the renewal period, has had adequate time to review these terms with legal and financial advisors, and enters into this renewal agreement with full knowledge and understanding of the material changes from the original agreement. Address how any special provisions, amendments, side letters, or modifications from the original agreement will be treated upon renewal—whether they will automatically carry forward into the new franchise agreement, be subject to renegotiation, or terminate upon expiration of the original agreement. This is particularly important for franchisees who may have negotiated favorable terms in the original agreement that differ from current system standards.

Clarify the exact date and time when the original agreement terminates and the new franchise agreement becomes effective, ensuring no gap in coverage that could create ambiguity about which agreement governs during any transition period. Specify that all obligations accrued under the original agreement survive its termination, including any indemnification obligations, confidentiality requirements, post-term non-competition covenants, and any amounts owed for periods prior to renewal. Address the treatment of any deposits, reserves, or funds held by the franchisor under the original agreement, such as grand opening advertising reserves or technology upgrade funds, and specify how they will be applied, credited, or refunded under the new agreement.

## Premises Upgrades and Modernization Requirements

Articulate detailed requirements for bringing the franchised premises and operations into compliance with current brand standards, image requirements, and trade dress specifications. The franchisee must complete, at its sole cost and expense, all upgrades, remodeling, refurbishment, and modernization of the franchised premises as specified by the franchisor to conform to the then-current image, design standards, color schemes, signage requirements, and operational layout of the franchise system. Specify a reasonable but firm timeframe for completion of these improvements, typically ranging from ninety to one hundred eighty days from the renewal effective date, with provisions for reasonable extensions based on circumstances genuinely beyond the franchisee's control such as delays in obtaining building permits, zoning approvals, or landlord consents.

Require the franchisee to submit detailed plans and specifications for all proposed renovation work to the franchisor for review and written approval before commencing any construction or remodeling activities, and establish the franchisor's right to require modifications to submitted plans to ensure compliance with brand standards. Include provisions granting the franchisor or its designated representatives the right to inspect the franchised premises during renovation and upon completion to verify compliance with approved plans and current brand standards. Address the consequences of failure to complete required improvements within the specified timeframe, which may include suspension of the renewal, termination of the franchise agreement, or the franchisor's right to complete the improvements at the franchisee's expense.

Specify the scope of required improvements with sufficient detail to avoid disputes about what is required versus what is optional. This may include exterior renovations such as new signage, facade improvements, parking lot resurfacing, and landscaping updates, as well as interior improvements such as new flooring, wall coverings, lighting fixtures, furniture, fixtures, and equipment. Address whether the franchisor will provide any financial assistance, favorable financing arrangements, or extended implementation timelines for significant capital investments, or whether the franchisee bears the entire financial burden of modernization as a condition of renewal.

## Equipment, Technology, and Operational System Updates

Require the franchisee to acquire, install, and implement any new equipment, technology systems, point-of-sale systems, inventory management systems, customer relationship management platforms, or operational procedures that have been adopted as mandatory system standards since the execution of the original agreement. This may include computer hardware and software, telecommunications systems, security and surveillance systems, kitchen equipment, food preparation equipment, or other operational tools necessary to maintain system uniformity, operational efficiency, and competitive positioning in the marketplace. Specify whether these technology and equipment upgrades must be completed before the renewal effective date or within a specified period thereafter, and address whether completion of these upgrades is a condition precedent to renewal or an ongoing obligation under the new franchise agreement.

Address whether the franchisor will provide any financial assistance, volume purchasing discounts, favorable financing terms through approved vendors, or extended implementation timelines for significant capital investments in new technology or equipment. Specify training requirements for the franchisee and its employees on new systems, procedures, menu items, service protocols, or customer service standards that have been introduced since the original franchise agreement was executed. Include provisions requiring the franchisee to attend and successfully complete any required retraining programs at the franchisor's training facility or through approved online training platforms, and address who bears the cost of such training, including tuition, travel, lodging, and wages for employees attending training.

## Release of Claims and Covenant Not to Sue

Draft a carefully worded provision requiring the franchisee and all of its owners to execute a comprehensive general release as a condition of renewal. This release should encompass all claims, demands, damages, liabilities, causes of action, and rights of any nature whatsoever, whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, that the franchisee or its owners may have against the franchisor, its parent company, subsidiaries, affiliates, officers, directors, shareholders, employees, agents, and representatives, arising from or relating to the franchise relationship, the original agreement, or any other agreements between the parties, from the inception of the relationship through the date of the release.

Include appropriate carve-outs for claims that cannot legally be released under applicable law, such as certain statutory rights that may exist under state franchise relationship laws, the Petroleum Marketing Practices Act, or other protective legislation. Some jurisdictions prohibit franchisors from requiring releases of certain claims as a condition of renewal, so research applicable state law to ensure your release provision complies with local requirements. Consider including mutual release language if appropriate to the circumstances and if the franchisor is willing to release any claims it may have against the franchisee arising from the original agreement, though franchisors typically require only the franchisee to provide a release.

Draft the release language broadly enough to accomplish the franchisor's objectives of achieving finality and preventing future litigation over past issues, while ensuring the language is not so overreaching that it would be deemed unconscionable or unenforceable by a court. Include acknowledgment language confirming that the franchisee has had the opportunity to consult with legal counsel regarding the release, understands its significance and legal effect, and executes it voluntarily with full knowledge of its rights.

## Representations and Warranties

Include comprehensive representations and warranties from the franchisee regarding matters material to the franchisor's decision to extend the franchise relationship. The franchisee should represent that it has full power and authority to enter into this renewal agreement, that the individual executing this agreement on behalf of the franchisee has actual authority to bind the entity, and that execution and performance of this agreement does not violate any other agreement to which the franchisee is a party or any court order or legal restriction applicable to the franchisee.

Require the franchisee to represent that all information provided to the franchisor in connection with the renewal, including financial statements, tax returns, and representations about compliance with the original agreement, is true, accurate, and complete in all material respects. Include representations that the franchisee has not assigned or transferred any interest in the original agreement or the franchised business except as previously disclosed to and approved in writing by the franchisor. Require representations that no litigation, arbitration, administrative proceedings, or governmental investigations are pending or threatened against the franchisee that would materially affect its ability to perform under the renewed franchise agreement or that involve allegations of violations of health codes, safety regulations, employment laws, or other matters that could reflect negatively on the franchise system.

Consider including representations regarding the franchisee's satisfaction with the franchise relationship and acknowledgment that the franchisor has fulfilled all of its material obligations under the original agreement. While such representations may seem favorable to the franchisor, they can be important in defending against future claims that the franchisor breached the original agreement or engaged in unfair or deceptive practices. Include representations that the franchisee has received all required disclosures under the Federal Trade Commission's Franchise Rule and applicable state franchise laws, has had adequate time to review these disclosures with advisors, and has not relied on any representations or promises not contained in the written disclosure documents or this renewal agreement.

## Governing Law, Dispute Resolution, and Enforcement Provisions

Specify the governing law that will apply to this renewal agreement, which should typically match the governing law provision in the new franchise agreement to ensure consistency in interpretation and enforcement. Include a clear statement that the laws of the specified state will govern without regard to its conflict of laws principles, which prevents a court from applying another state's law based on conflict of laws analysis. Address whether any specific state or federal franchise relationship laws will apply to the interpretation or enforcement of this agreement, and include appropriate disclaimers or acknowledgments regarding the application of such laws.

Draft comprehensive dispute resolution provisions that establish the exclusive mechanism for resolving disputes arising from or related to this renewal agreement. Specify whether disputes will be resolved through litigation in courts of specified jurisdiction, binding arbitration under specified rules (such as the American Arbitration Association's Commercial Arbitration Rules), mediation as a prerequisite to formal proceedings, or some combination of these mechanisms. If arbitration is required, specify the number of arbitrators, the method of selecting arbitrators, the location where arbitration will be conducted, and whether the arbitrator's authority is limited in any way (such as prohibiting punitive damages or class-wide relief).

Address choice of venue and jurisdiction for any legal proceedings, specifying the exclusive venue for litigation or arbitration and requiring the franchisee to consent to personal jurisdiction in the specified forum. Include provisions regarding the prevailing party's right to recover reasonable attorneys' fees, costs, and expenses incurred in connection with any dispute, which can serve as a deterrent to frivolous claims and ensure that the party whose position is vindicated is made whole. Consider including a requirement for the parties to engage in good faith negotiations or non-binding mediation before pursuing formal legal action, which can facilitate resolution of disputes without the expense and acrimony of litigation or arbitration.

Include provisions addressing the enforcement of this renewal agreement, such as the franchisee's acknowledgment that monetary damages may be inadequate to remedy breaches of certain provisions (such as confidentiality obligations or non-competition covenants) and that the franchisor is entitled to seek injunctive relief without posting a bond. Address the standard of proof or burden of persuasion that will apply in any dispute, and consider including provisions that shift certain burdens to the franchisee, such as requiring the franchisee to prove that it satisfied all conditions precedent to renewal if the franchisor later challenges the validity of the renewal.

## Integration, Amendment, and Miscellaneous Provisions

Draft a comprehensive integration clause stating that this renewal agreement, together with the new franchise agreement to be executed upon renewal and any exhibits or schedules attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, representations, and agreements, whether written or oral. Include language disclaiming reliance on any representations or promises not contained in this written agreement, which can be critical in defending against fraud or misrepresentation claims based on alleged oral promises made during renewal negotiations.

Address amendment procedures by specifying that any modifications to this renewal agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties, and that no course of dealing, course of performance, or trade usage will be used to modify or interpret the express terms of this agreement. Include severability language providing that if any provision of this agreement is found invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect, and the invalid provision will be reformed to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable while preserving the parties' original intent.

Add detailed provisions regarding notices, specifying acceptable methods of delivery (personal delivery, overnight courier, certified mail return receipt requested, email to specified addresses), the addresses to which notices should be sent for each party, and when notices are deemed received based on the method of delivery. Include provisions allowing either party to change its notice address by providing written notice to the other party in accordance with the notice provisions. Address waiver by specifying that no waiver of any provision or default under this agreement will be effective unless in writing and signed by the party against whom the waiver is sought to be enforced, and that no waiver of any provision or default will constitute a waiver of any other provision or default.

Include standard provisions regarding counterparts, stating that this agreement may be executed in multiple counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original and all of which together will constitute one and the same instrument. Address whether electronic signatures or PDF signatures will be deemed valid and enforceable to the same extent as original signatures. Include provisions regarding the binding effect of this agreement on the parties' successors and assigns, while clarifying that the franchisee may not assign this agreement or any rights hereunder without the franchisor's prior written consent.

## Signature Blocks and Execution Formalities

Conclude your draft with formal signature blocks for both parties that include spaces for the printed names, titles, and signatures of authorized representatives, along with the date of execution. For corporate entities, include appropriate attestation lines and corporate seal requirements if applicable under the law of the state of incorporation. Ensure that the signature authority is consistent with the entity type—for corporations, the president, vice president, or other authorized officer should sign; for limited liability companies, a member or manager should sign; for partnerships, a general partner should sign.

Include acknowledgment language above the signature blocks confirming that the signing party has read this agreement, understands its terms, has had the opportunity to consult with legal and financial advisors, and executes it voluntarily. Consider including specific acknowledgments regarding material terms such as the release of claims, the requirement to execute the current form of franchise agreement with potentially different terms, and the obligation to complete premises upgrades at the franchisee's expense. For individual guarantors, include separate signature blocks with appropriate guarantee language confirming their personal liability for the franchisee's obligations under this renewal agreement and the new franchise agreement.

## Legal and Regulatory Compliance Considerations

Ensure your draft complies with all applicable federal and state franchise relationship laws, including the Federal Trade Commission's Franchise Rule (16 C.F.R. Part 436) and any applicable state franchise registration and relationship laws. Be particularly attentive to state laws that may restrict a franchisor's ability to impose new terms upon renewal, require good cause for refusing to renew, grant franchisees statutory renewal rights, or prohibit certain provisions in franchise agreements. Research the specific requirements of states with comprehensive franchise relationship laws, such as California, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Michigan, Minnesota, Nebraska, New Jersey, Washington, and Wisconsin, as well as states with petroleum franchise laws and automobile dealer franchise laws.

Review whether the renewal triggers any disclosure obligations under the FTC Franchise Rule or state franchise laws. While renewals are generally exempt from the FTC Rule's disclosure requirements if no material changes are made to the franchise relationship, material changes in terms may trigger disclosure obligations. Ensure that if disclosure is required, the franchisee has received the current Franchise Disclosure Document at least fourteen calendar days before executing this renewal agreement or making any payment to the franchisor. Address any state-specific disclosure requirements, which may differ from federal requirements in timing, content, or triggering events.

Consider the implications of any material changes in terms between the original agreement and the new franchise agreement, particularly regarding royalty rates, territorial rights, operational requirements, or non-competition covenants. Some jurisdictions impose restrictions on a franchisor's ability to materially change terms upon renewal without good cause or without providing adequate notice and opportunity to cure. Research whether the specific changes contemplated in this renewal would be permissible under applicable state franchise relationship laws, and consider whether any grandfathering provisions or transition periods should be included to ease the franchisee's adjustment to new requirements.

Address any industry-specific regulations that may affect the franchised business, such as health and safety requirements for food service franchises, environmental regulations for automotive service franchises, labor laws affecting multi-unit operators, or licensing requirements for professional service franchises. Ensure that the renewal agreement does not require the franchisee to violate any applicable laws or regulations, and consider including provisions requiring the franchisee to maintain compliance with all applicable laws as a condition of the renewed franchise relationship.

## Expected Output Format and Professional Presentation

Present your final draft as a formal legal agreement with numbered sections and subsections for easy reference and navigation. Use clear, descriptive headings and subheadings to organize the content logically and allow readers to quickly locate specific provisions. Employ defined terms consistently throughout the document, with initial definitions capitalized and used uniformly thereafter to avoid confusion. Maintain a professional, formal tone appropriate for a binding commercial contract while ensuring readability and comprehension by business people who may not have legal training.

Format the document with appropriate spacing, margins, and typography to enhance readability and professional appearance. Use a standard professional font such as Times New Roman, Arial, or Calibri in eleven or twelve-point size for body text, with larger sizes for headings and subheadings. Number all pages consecutively and consider including headers or footers with the document title, date, and page numbers. Leave adequate space in the signature blocks for actual signatures and dates, and ensure that signature blocks do not appear alone on a page separated from the provisions they relate to.

Your completed draft should typically range from ten to eighteen pages depending on the complexity of the franchise relationship, the number and scope of conditions precedent to renewal, the extent of premises upgrades and modernization required, and any special provisions necessitated by the franchisee's performance history or unique circumstances. Prioritize clarity, precision, and enforceability over brevity, ensuring that all material terms are addressed comprehensively and that ambiguity is eliminated to the greatest extent possible. The goal is to create a document that clearly establishes the parties' rights and obligations, minimizes the potential for future disputes, and provides a solid legal foundation for the renewed franchise relationship.
